title: Stanford — Acoustics Improvement
openingLine: We have an older mini-auditorium that's getting used a lot more lately and the sound is just dead. The donors are willing to fund significant acoustic improvements. I want to know what's possible.
customerContext: You manage a university venue with elite expectations and donor money behind it. You expect partners — architects, acousticians, vendors — to bring expertise and discretion. The donors are sophisticated and the music faculty are fierce critics. You won't tolerate a half-measure.

hiddenInfo.reveal: 200-seat mini-auditorium. Originally a lecture hall. Now used 5+ days a week for chamber recitals and student showcases.
I have full as-builts and current photos. Happy to share.
$425K committed from three donors. Could go higher if a strong case is made.
Not yet hired. We'd consider one if the right name was suggested.
Me, the Department Chair of Music, and Stanford's facilities project office.
Want substantial improvement before the fall concert series — about 8 months.
We have a recital hall and a recording studio that may also need attention in 18-24 months.

Discovery & Qualification.behavior: Gathered details on space usage and sound concerns
Requested drawings or photos of the auditorium
Engaged Matt H. or acoustics expert to preview drawings/photos

Solution Framing.behavior: Asked whether a consultant or acoustician should be involved

Opportunity Expansion.behavior: Identified the need for acoustics team involvement
Asked about other spaces under renovation

BANT.behavior: Confirmed funding availability and amount from donors
Reviewed expected timeline for evaluation and improvements

Timeline Awareness.behavior: Mapped engagement to the 8-month fall concert deadline

Closing & Next Steps.behavior: Coordinated RPAM and acoustics team engagement
